D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Primary & Secondary Rainbows — Descartes Geometry

Primary rainbow: one internal reflection inside the drop, peaks at 42° from antisolar point; secondary: two internal reflections, 51° with reversed colour order — dark Alexander's band between

Newton solved this in 1666. For each wavelength, calculate the angle of minimum deviation (∂D/∂θ_i = 0). Result: red appears on the outer edge of the primary (42.4°), violet on the inner (40.7°); secondary reverses (50.6° violet outer, 53.4° red inner) because of the extra reflection.

Slide ray's impact parameter b/R from rim to centre to trace the cone of deviation.
